summary.overview:
- **Lifecycle Survey Launch:** Final version to go live in about seven business days after question confirmation. 

- **Confidential Surveys:** Surveys will remain anonymous, ensuring respondent confidentiality, similar to Glint surveys.

- **Survey Logic Limitations:** Current surveys don’t support conditional logic; future plans include improving survey distribution based on attributes.

- **Data Integration Complete:** Employee survey data integration confirmed; candidate survey integration still needs discussion and clarification.

- **Quality Assurance Checks:** Thorough reviews of survey content and communication templates are essential before launch to minimize risks.

- **Lessons Learned Documentation:** All feedback, process changes, and lessons will be recorded to guide future survey initiatives and improvements.
summary.action_items:
**Kate Burk**
Review and confirm lifecycle survey questions today post-meeting to enable survey activation (16:53)

**Dayna Matusek**
Finish detailed review of survey stages configuration and questions alongside Kate today (16:53)

**Yugi P**
Provide survey invitation email template previews to Kate and Dana within next 1-2 hours post-meeting (19:37)

**Scott Domes**
Follow up with Sonia and relevant parties on survey data access permissions and reporting controls (24:03)

**Patrick Briscoe**
Participate in candidate data integration discussions in dedicated call tomorrow (31:13)
summary.notes:
## **Lifecycle Survey Deployment**

The team agreed to finalize and launch the Lifecycle surveys within the next week after question confirmation.

- **Kate Burk and Dayna Matusek committed to reviewing and confirming survey questions by end of day**, aiming for a **go-live date approximately seven business days after approval** (12:53).
    - Yugi P confirmed that once questions are approved, engineering will deploy the surveys and automation will start sending surveys automatically to eligible employees.
    - The surveys cover **5-day, 30-day, and 90-day milestones** with consistent question sets including **NPS and open text feedback**.
    - The surveys will remain **anonymous**, aligning with current Glint survey practice to preserve respondent confidentiality.
    - This deployment is expected to provide ongoing employee feedback to improve onboarding and engagement.

- **The survey reminder schedule was set to send reminders on Fridays for all survey waves** to maximize response rates shortly after survey triggers (18:45).
    - This timing matches current Glint settings and helps ensure new hires receive timely nudges to complete surveys.
    - Kate and Dayna clarified the onboarding email messages and survey landing page introductions will be personalized, while the thank you page remains generic.

- **Survey logic functionality is currently limited to static flows without conditional question branching** (14:44).
    - Yugi explained conditional logic for question-level branching is not yet supported but is planned for future development.
    - Instead, conditional survey distribution will be handled by sending different surveys based on attributes like assessment completion or interview rounds.
    - Kate will compile feedback on the need for conditional logic and share it with Scott Domes and Charlie for roadmap consideration.

## **Integration and Data Access**

The Lifecycle survey integration is largely resolved on the employee side, with candidate survey integration remaining under discussion.

- **Patrick Briscoe confirmed that employee Lifecycle survey data integration is complete from an API perspective**, with join date and other key fields correctly flowing (14:10).
    - Candidate survey integration still has unresolved questions, to be addressed in a separate meeting with Karthik Rao.

- **Survey data visibility and permissions were clarified with a focus on anonymous results for Lifecycle surveys** (24:08).
    - Anonymous surveys prevent viewing individual respondent identities even by super admins, aligning with current Glint privacy standards.
    - Super admins can access aggregated dashboards but cannot extract confidential reports unless surveys are configured as confidential (which Lifecycle surveys are not).
    - Scott will follow up on permissions with relevant stakeholders to confirm proper access controls.

- **Yugi will provide separate documentation illustrating reporting views for employee Lifecycle surveys distinct from candidate surveys**, reflecting differences in recruiter data availability (31:45).
    - Candidate Lifecycle surveys include “primary recruiter” roles that do not apply to employee surveys, requiring tailored report views.

## **Survey Content and Quality Assurance**

The team emphasized thorough review of survey content and communication templates to maintain message quality and minimize risk.

- **Kate and Dayna will review survey questions and email communication templates thoroughly, aiming for completion within hours to a day** (16:53).
    - Yugi will provide final email template previews within two hours for review.
    - The thank you email message will be shared to ensure alignment across documentation and communications.

- **Scott Domes requested a 30-minute preview session with Charlie and Catherine to validate messaging before launch** (22:31).
    - This step follows historical practice to catch any issues and avoid negative reactions.
    - Kate and Dayna will coordinate with Charlie and Catherine to confirm if the preview is needed.

- **Survey messaging has been previously approved by Terry K, with Kate planning a one-on-one check-in to confirm no new concerns** (22:09).
    - This reassures leadership alignment and reduces risk of last-minute messaging issues.

## **Candidate Survey and Recruiter Attribution**

Candidate survey complexities including recruiter attribution require further discussion outside this meeting.

- **Kate highlighted the need to associate contract recruiters with candidate survey scores**, noting current employee Lifecycle surveys lack a “primary recruiter” field (30:50).
    - Yugi confirmed employee surveys only track reporting managers, not recruiters.
    - A dedicated meeting with candidate survey lead Karthik Rao is scheduled to address these gaps separately.

- **This distinction means candidate Lifecycle reports will differ from employee Lifecycle reports**, requiring separate documentation and potentially different integration work (31:43).
    - Yugi will send tailored employee Lifecycle survey report documentation to clarify capabilities.
    - Patrick and others will join subsequent calls to resolve candidate survey integration and recruiter mapping.

## **Process and Lessons Learned**

The team agreed to document ongoing feedback and lessons learned to improve future survey initiatives.

- **Scott Domes requested capturing any capability, process, or feedback changes in the project file as lessons learned** to avoid losing details and guide future efforts (32:27).
    - Kate will submit conditional logic requests and other feedback via Slack as part of this documentation.
    - This approach ensures continuous improvement and efficient tracking of open issues for both Lifecycle and candidate surveys.

- **Scott emphasized the importance of maintaining clear communication and quality checks as the surveys approach launch**, reflecting past experiences with survey rollouts (22:43).
    - The team’s coordinated review and follow-up steps aim to mitigate risks and maintain high standards.
